content.claim: Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks are concentrated in urban and county-seat locations (59 schools in Budapest, 63 in provincial cities, 121 in small towns, only 8 in villages), structurally insulating them from rural depopulation while ordinary comprehensive schools face sharper enrollment pressure
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Based on current geographic snapshot, not projections of future urban migration patterns
Does not quantify differential closure risk between track types under specific demographic scenarios
Fieldwork methodology not fully specified in notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
